concerns.text: FR-003 mandates three distinct prompt conditions: Monolithic, Separated Tracks, and Generic Baseline. The tasks (T008, T012, T014) and plan only implement two conditions (Monolithic, Separated). The 'Generic Baseline' condition is missing from the implementation tasks, leaving FR-003 unfulfilled.

FR-002 requires generating a global pool of tasks distributed across 'multiple specific task domains (coding, math, logic, creative, factual)' for '50 profiles'. Tasks T006 and T007 only generate 10 profiles and 50 tasks total, with no explicit task to enforce the stratified domain distribution or the 50-profile scale required by the spec.

FR-006 specifies a 'Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM)'. The tasks (T028) and plan implement a 'Generalized Linear Mixed Model (GLMM)'. While the plan justifies this in 'Complexity Tracking', there is no task to update the spec or explicitly map the GLMM implementation to the FR-006 requirement, creating a traceability gap for the requirement itself.

SC-003 requires measuring total compute time against the free-tier runner limit. The plan estimates ~2 hours (7,500s) per job, likely exceeding standard 3600s CI timeouts. There is no task in the implementation plan to segment the workload, handle timeouts, or explicitly measure and report this metric against the limit as required by SC-003.

FR-008 requires reporting the 'variance in false-positive rates' for the sensitivity analysis. Task T024 only mentions 'logging false-positive variance' without defining the output artifact or metric format required to verify this specific criterion, leaving the reporting requirement unimplemented.
